attempt: Transfer Ticket 37's lesson: a bounded rank candidate becomes useful only if it names the pointwise extension theorem. Aggregate evidence and density controls remain support, not proof.

bounded_result.bounded_rank_analogue: A cutoff rank must give a positive representation lower bound for each even N beyond N0.
bounded_result.discarded_route: density-positive or almost-all Goldbach theorem treated as pointwise Goldbach proof
bounded_result.retained_route: explicit lower bound with a finite verified seed interval

obstruction: The remaining obstruction is pointwise. Sparse exceptional objects can survive every bounded or averaged test unless a rank or positivity theorem excludes each one.
candidate_theorem: There is an explicit N0 such that every even N>N0 has a positive binary-prime representation count, with all N<=N0 verified.
next_experiment: Search for sparse even-number adversaries that pass density checks but minimize pointwise representation counts.
claim_boundary: No goldbach proof and no certified goldbach counterexample.
